# Transcrição: 05-Integrate Culturally

- **Fonte:** 05-Integrate Culturally
- **Duração:** 05:02
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

This video will focus on cultural integration. Most acquisitions, mergers, or consolidations in the business world usually ignore cultural integration and instead only focus on operational and financial integration. I promise you this is a huge, huge mistake because cultural integration is the number one driver for your business's success. Cultural integration aligns your purpose with the team's purpose. Specifically, you need to know how aligned your new employees are with your mission personally, professionally, financially. The more you can align personal, professional, and financial mission together and the business wins, your people win. When your people win, the business wins. If you ignore that and they think they're working for you to pay off your bank debt, you're going to have disengaged employees. At the company I founded and exited, Otagee, the core leadership team was roughly 60% of the people who had their first job working with me. They've been there for 15 years. That's a team that was culturally integrated and aligned personally, professionally, and financially. That's why they stayed. Now, some of you might be saying, I didn't buy this business. I don't know what's going to happen in 15 years. You know what? I didn't know 15 years ago either exactly what was going to happen. But I treated those people as though when I do have something happen, I want to make sure they're there, they're aligned, and they're giving me everything they got while they are there all 15 years. As a consequence, they're still working with me in the businesses because they were fully aligned. They were able to grow personally, professionally, financially, grow their acumen, grow their contribution, grow most importantly their confidence in their ability to succeed. Your responsibility is to create that for the people who work in the new business that you just bought. Now, we were able to do that because we were always being very proactive about their personal, professional, financial goals. And we were working to align them with the goals of the business. It was part of our planning process. You can tell somebody to do a job and they might go do it. But they'll be working with a different level of engagement if they're inspired to go out and crush those goals because they're totally aligned with the mission of the organization. You see, when you can align individuals goals with the mission of your organization, personally, professionally, and financially, you're going to get a lot more horsepower. And more importantly, you're going to get active engagement with people who are focused on building your business. The focus is on self-awareness of the team members. It's on team communication. It's on interpersonal relationships. Consider having a meeting every day, every week, every month, every quarter, and every year. Communicating the company's targets, the wins that the company

## [03:01 - 05:02]

has, the successes that you're searching for. Re-establish every single time the company's mission, vision, and values and why you do what you do and who you want to do it with and what you want to accomplish. One of the ways that we help our business owners is through a program we call R3. Write people, write place, write expectations. We do this first by understanding the personality and the contribution of each employee to the group. Now, if you're focused on learning about people and having your people learn about themselves and your intention is to always help your people be the best version of themselves while they're at work, guess what? You're not only improving their professional life, but you're improving all their lives. And when you improve the lives of the people who trust you, follow you, and work with you, they give you more attention on what allows you to be successful. When you share that success with them, they will lean in and build your business to another level of success because they want to prove to you and show you they appreciate you. How do you do this? You have to identify who your leadership team will be, teach them these concepts, and then teach them to design a duplication plan for top performers. Teach them how to find, attract, align, develop, and retain top performers. Success breeds success. The more success that you can show people that they can accomplish working with you, the more they'll go out, recruit people, bring them into the system because you taught them how to create success. This becomes, I'm just going to shortcut you now that you bought this new business. This is how you create a remarkable value in business is by tapping in and harnessing the horsepower of your team. So they'll help you build your business to the highest value possible. When the business wins, they win. When they win, the business wins.
